questions.q: What is delimitation?
options: The process of redrawing the boundaries of electoral constituencies
The process of counting votes after an election
The process of registering political parties
The process of appointing the Prime Minister
questions.explanation: Delimitation is the act of redrawing the boundaries of electoral constituencies to reflect changes in population.

questions.q: Delimitation in India is mainly carried out to account for changes in what?
questions.explanation: Delimitation redraws constituency boundaries to reflect changes in population.

questions.q: Which body carries out delimitation of constituencies in India?
questions.explanation: An independent Delimitation Commission is established to carry out the delimitation exercise.

questions.q: How is the Delimitation Commission set up?
options: By a decision of a single state
questions.explanation: The Delimitation Commission is established by an Act of Parliament.

questions.q: Delimitation determines the boundaries of constituencies for which kinds of bodies?
options: Legislative bodies such as the Lok Sabha and state assemblies
questions.explanation: Delimitation fixes the boundaries of parliamentary and assembly constituencies.

questions.q: The data on population used for delimitation is primarily drawn from which exercise?
questions.explanation: Delimitation uses Census figures to reflect population changes across constituencies.

questions.q: A key aim of delimitation is to ensure that constituencies are roughly equal in what respect?
questions.explanation: Delimitation seeks to make constituencies comparable in population so that votes carry similar weight.

questions.q: Delimitation also involves reserving certain constituencies for which groups?
questions.explanation: The delimitation process identifies constituencies reserved for Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes.

questions.q: Orders issued by the Delimitation Commission generally have what status before the law?
options: They have the force of law and cannot be questioned in any court
They must be approved by every state assembly first
questions.explanation: Under the law governing delimitation, the Commission's orders have the force of law and are not open to challenge in court.

questions.q: Delimitation is most directly connected to which broader democratic activity?
questions.explanation: Delimitation defines the constituencies in which elections are held.

questions.q: Which Articles of the Constitution deal with the readjustment of seats and division of states into constituencies after each census?
questions.explanation: Article 82 provides for readjustment of Lok Sabha seats and Article 170 for state legislative assembly constituencies after each census.

questions.q: How many Delimitation Commissions have been constituted in India under dedicated delimitation Acts?
questions.explanation: Delimitation Commissions were set up in 1952, 1962, 1972 and 2002, making four in total.

questions.q: Who typically heads a Delimitation Commission in India?
options: A serving or retired judge of the Supreme Court
The Speaker of the Lok Sabha
questions.explanation: A Delimitation Commission is usually chaired by a serving or retired Supreme Court judge.

questions.q: The Chief Election Commissioner serves on the Delimitation Commission in what capacity?
options: He has no role at all
questions.explanation: The Chief Election Commissioner is an ex-officio member of the Delimitation Commission.

questions.q: The most recent Delimitation Commission set up under a delimitation Act was constituted in which year?
questions.explanation: The Delimitation Commission constituted under the Delimitation Act of 2002 was the most recent such commission.

questions.q: By which constitutional amendment was the total number of Lok Sabha seats among the states frozen, based on the 1971 census, until the first census after 2000?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment of 1976 froze the allocation of Lok Sabha seats to states on the basis of the 1971 census.

questions.q: By which amendment was the freeze on the number of seats among the states further extended until the first census taken after 2026?
questions.explanation: The 84th Amendment of 2001 extended the freeze on inter-state seat allocation until the first census after 2026.

questions.q: Besides Articles 82 and 170, which Articles empower Parliament to make laws determining the constitution of, and readjustments to, the Houses?
questions.explanation: Article 81 deals with the composition of the Lok Sabha and works alongside Articles 82 and 170 in the delimitation framework.

questions.q: Under the 84th Amendment, what was permitted to be readjusted within states even while the total number of seats remained frozen?
options: The boundaries of constituencies based on the 2001 census
The duration of the Lok Sabha term
questions.explanation: The 84th Amendment allowed intra-state readjustment of constituency boundaries on the basis of the 2001 census without changing each state's total seats.

questions.q: Decisions of a Delimitation Commission are made how when members differ?
options: By referring the matter to Parliament
By a majority of the members
questions.explanation: Where members of the Delimitation Commission differ, decisions are taken by majority.

questions.q: Associate members on a Delimitation Commission, drawn from Parliament and state legislatures, have what kind of role?
options: They participate but have no right to vote
questions.explanation: Associate members assist the Commission but do not have a vote in its decisions.

questions.q: The first delimitation exercise in independent India was associated with which Commission, set up in the early 1950s?
questions.explanation: The first Delimitation Commission was constituted in 1952 to draw constituencies for early general elections.

questions.q: The Delimitation Commission Act under which the most recent commission worked was passed in which year?
questions.explanation: The Delimitation Act, 2002, governed the work of the most recent Delimitation Commission.

questions.q: The delimitation exercise carried out under the 2002 Act was based primarily on the census of which year?
questions.explanation: That delimitation exercise used the 2001 census figures for redrawing constituencies within states.

questions.q: Who chaired the Delimitation Commission constituted under the Delimitation Act of 2002?
questions.explanation: The Delimitation Commission set up under the 2002 Act was headed by Justice Kuldip Singh, a former Supreme Court judge.

questions.q: Due to security concerns, the delimitation exercise of the 2000s was deferred in which states at that time?
options: Assam, Arunachal Pradesh, Manipur and Nagaland
questions.explanation: Delimitation was deferred in Assam, Arunachal Pradesh, Manipur and Nagaland due to circumstances prevailing in those states.

questions.q: The 84th Amendment, which extended the seat freeze, was enacted in which year?
questions.explanation: The 84th Constitutional Amendment was enacted in 2001.

questions.q: Following the reorganisation that created Telangana, a separate delimitation of constituencies for that state and Andhra Pradesh was addressed primarily through which mechanism?
options: The Andhra Pradesh Reorganisation Act provisions
questions.explanation: Matters of constituency arrangement following Telangana's creation were dealt with under the Andhra Pradesh Reorganisation Act framework.

questions.q: A delimitation exercise for which Union Territory was conducted by a dedicated Delimitation Commission constituted in 2020?
questions.explanation: A Delimitation Commission was constituted in 2020 to delimit constituencies in the Union Territory of Jammu and Kashmir.

questions.q: Under the constitutional scheme, until the readjustment takes effect, the existing division of constituencies continues so as not to affect what?
options: The representation in the House until its dissolution
questions.explanation: The Constitution provides that readjustment does not disturb existing representation in a House until it is dissolved.
